name: Harvest Upside-Down Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
2. Melt butter in a heavy 10-inch oven-proof skillet or a 9x9x2 inch square pan.
3. Sprinkle brown sugar evenly over the melted butter.
4. Arrange apple slices in a row around the edge of the skillet.
5. Place remaining apple slices in two more rows within the skillet.
6. Spoon cranberry sauce between the rows.
7. In a large bowl, mix flour, granulated sugar, baking powder, and salt.
8. Add shortening, milk, and vanilla to the bowl; beat on medium speed for 2 minutes, scraping the bowl constantly.
9. Add egg and continue beating on medium speed for another 2 minutes, scraping the bowl frequently.
10. Pour the batter over the fruit in the skillet.
11. Bake for about 50 minutes until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
12. Immediately invert the skillet onto a heatproof serving plate.
13. Allow the sugar mixture to run down over the cake; serve warm with whipped cream or vanilla ice cream if desired.
